AMP: Moreover, it is [essentially] required of stewards that a man should be found faithful [proving himself worthy of trust].

ET a man so account of us, as of the ministers of .Christ, and stewards of the mysteries of God.

AMP: Now I have applied all this [about parties and factions] to myself and Apollos for your sakes, brethren, so that from what I have said of us [as illustrations], you may learn [to think of men in accordance with Scripture and] not to go beyond that which is written, that none of you may be puffed

AMP: When we are slandered and defamed, we [try to] answer softly and bring comfort. We have been made and are now the rubbish and filth of the world [the offscouring of all things, the scum of the earth].

puffed up (efusiwvqhsavn) be conceited/arrogant Some, supposing Paul would not come himself, have become inflated with self-importance.
puffed up (Greek phusioo) to make natural; to bear one's self loftily, be proud
The Introductory Hymn First Strophe In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
